skill.name: azure-enterprise-infra-planner
skill.description: Architect and provision enterprise Azure infrastructure from workload descriptions. For cloud architects and platform engineers planning networking, identity, security, compliance, and multi-resource topologies with WAF alignment.
skill.summary: Design and deploy enterprise Azure infrastructure with WAF-aligned best practices, generating Bicep or Terraform IaC.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static scanner detected 1102 pattern matches across 35 documentation files (2013 lines). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES: the scanner misinterpreted markdown code blocks (backticks) as shell execution, and documentation URLs/IPs as hardcoded credentials. This skill uses Azure MCP tools and CLI commands legitimately for infrastructure planning. Risk rated LOW due to external_commands risk factor from legitimate az/terraform CLI usage.

content.user_title: Architect enterprise Azure infrastructure with WAF alignment
content.value_statement: Cloud architects struggle to design compliant Azure topologies with proper networking, security, and disaster recovery. This skill generates WAF-aligned infrastructure plans and IaC (Bicep or Terraform) from workload descriptions.
actual_capabilities: Generate infrastructure plans from workload descriptions with WAF alignment
Design hub-spoke networks, VNets, firewalls, and private endpoints
Plan multi-region disaster recovery and high-availability topologies
Generate Bicep or Terraform IaC files from approved plans
Validate IaC using az bicep build or terraform validate
Deploy infrastructure at subscription or resource group scope
limitations: Requires Azure MCP tools for best practices lookup (get_azure_bestpractices, wellarchitectedframework_serviceguide_get)
Does not support azd (Azure Developer CLI) workflows - uses direct Bicep or Terraform
Plan approval required before IaC generation or deployment
Destructive actions require explicit user confirmation

use_cases.title: Enterprise landing zone design
use_cases.description: Platform engineers design a hub-spoke network topology with proper identity, RBAC, and compliance controls for a new enterprise workload.

use_cases.title: Multi-region DR topology
use_cases.description: Cloud architects plan cross-region high-availability infrastructure with failover capabilities and disaster recovery procedures.

use_cases.title: Subscription-scope deployment
use_cases.description: DevOps engineers generate and deploy Bicep modules across multiple resource groups at subscription scope.

prompt_templates.title: Basic infrastructure plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an Azure infrastructure for a web application with App Service, Azure SQL, and Application Gateway. Use WAF best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Hub-spoke network design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a hub-spoke network topology with Azure Firewall in the hub, VNets for dev/staging/prod in spokes, and private endpoints for data services. Generate Bicep IaC.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-region DR topology
prompt_templates.prompt: Architect a multi-region disaster recovery solution with active-passive failover between eastus and westus. Include Traffic Manager, paired regions, and backup strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Full IaC generation and deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Terraform IaC for a data analytics platform with Synapse, Data Lake Storage, and Event Hubs. Validate and deploy to subscription scope after my approval.

output_examples.input: Plan a cost-optimized event-driven pipeline with Container Apps, Event Grid, and Cosmos DB
output_examples.output: Infrastructure plan JSON with resources: resource group, Log Analytics workspace, managed identity, Container Apps environment, Event Grid domain/topic subscription, Cosmos DB account with serverless capacity. All resources tagged with CAF naming conventions and WAF-aligned configurations.

output_examples.input: Generate Bicep for approved hub-spoke network plan
output_examples.output: infra/main.bicep orchestrating modules: networking.bicep (VNet, subnets, route tables), security.bicep (Azure Firewall, NSGs), identity.bicep (managed identity). Includes main.bicepparam with environment-specific values.

best_practices: Always call get_azure_bestpractices and wellarchitectedframework_serviceguide_get MCP tools before planning to ensure WAF alignment
Generate infrastructure plan JSON first and get user approval before creating any IaC files
Use managed identity for service-to-service authentication instead of connection strings or keys
anti_patterns: Do not generate IaC without an approved infrastructure plan - plan first, code second
Do not place Bicep files in the project root - always use infra/ directory with modules/ subdirectory
Do not deploy without confirming subscription, resource group, and deployment scope with the user

faq.question: When should I use this skill instead of azure-prepare?
faq.answer: Use azure-enterprise-infra-planner for infrastructure-focused workloads (networking, landing zones, multi-resource topologies). Use azure-prepare for application-centric deployments where the focus is on deploying a specific app to Azure.

faq.question: Does this skill support both Bicep and Terraform?
faq.answer: Yes. After plan approval, you will be asked to choose Bicep or Terraform. Both generate equivalent infrastructure under infra/ directory.

faq.question: What deployment scopes are supported?
faq.answer: Resource group scope (az deployment group create) and subscription scope (az deployment sub create). The skill determines scope based on targetScope in main.bicep.

faq.question: How does WAF alignment work?
faq.answer: The skill calls MCP tools to fetch Azure best practices and WAF service guides, then applies the five WAF pillars (cost optimization, operational excellence, performance efficiency, reliability, security) to resource selection and configuration.

faq.question: Can I modify the generated infrastructure plan?
faq.answer: Yes. The plan JSON is written to .azure/infrastructure-plan.json. You can review and request changes before approval. Once approved, status changes to 'approved' and IaC generation proceeds.

faq.question: What MCP tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: get_azure_bestpractices, wellarchitectedframework_serviceguide_get, microsoft_docs_search, microsoft_docs_fetch, and bicepschema_get. If tools are unavailable, the skill falls back to reference files.
